name: Concrete Laser Cleaning Dataset
description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for Concrete. Includes thermal, optical, mechanical, and laser interaction properties validated against industry standards.

license.name: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International
license.description: Free to share and adapt with attribution

measurementTechnique: Laser ablation testing, material characterization, spectroscopy

includedInDataCatalog.name: Z-Beam Material Properties Database
includedInDataCatalog.description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for industrial applications

powerRange.description: Optimal average power for concrete surface cleaning and contaminant removal

wavelength.description: Near-infrared wavelength optimized for concrete absorption and minimal substrate damage

spotSize.description: Beam spot diameter for effective cleaning coverage and energy density control

repetitionRate.description: Optimal repetition rate for continuous cleaning coverage without thermal buildup

energyDensity.description: Fluence threshold for effective contaminant removal while preserving concrete integrity

pulseWidth.description: Pulse duration balancing efficient contaminant removal with concrete substrate preservation

scanSpeed.description: Optimal scanning speed for uniform cleaning with sufficient energy deposition

passCount.description: Recommended number of passes for complete contaminant removal

overlapRatio.description: Optimal beam overlap percentage for uniform cleaning coverage

power.description: Typical range for industrial 1064 nm laser cleaning of concrete surfaces (e.g., graffiti or rust removal); starting at 500 W balances efficiency and safety to avoid thermal cracking. Max safe below substrate damage threshold (~1000-1500 W depending on fluence). Source: CleanLaser and IPG Photonics equipment specs; academic review in J. Laser Appl. (2018) on stone/concrete cleaning.

scan_speed.description: Effective range for contamination removal on concrete; slower (100-500 mm/s) for heavy soiling like rust, faster (2000-5000 mm/s) for light contaminants. Recommended 1000 mm/s for optimal balance of cleaning rate and uniformity, considering concrete's low thermal conductivity. Source: Industry standards from Laser Clean Tech manuals; case study in Opt. Lasers Eng. (2020).

pulse_width.description: Nanosecond regime (10-150 ns) typical for concrete to enable ablation with minimal heat-affected zone (HAZ); picosecond (ps) or femtosecond (fs) reduces HAZ further but increases cost. Recommended 50 ns for efficient contaminant removal without cracking porous concrete. Source: Laser physics research in Appl. Surf. Sci. (2019) on pulsed laser ablation of cementitious materials.

repetition_rate.description: Standard range for 1064 nm cleaning; higher rates (50-100 kHz) speed up process but risk heat accumulation in low-conductivity concrete. Recommended 50 kHz for balanced throughput and thermal management. Source: Academic literature in J. Clean. Prod. (2021) on industrial laser parameters for building materials.

energy_density.description: Fluence range for safe contaminant ablation (e.g., paints or oxides); concrete damage threshold ~8-12 J/cm² to prevent spalling. Recommended 2 J/cm² for effective cleaning below threshold, accounting for variable reflectivity at 1064 nm. Source: Ablation threshold studies in Surf. Coat. Technol. (2017) and Opt. Express (2022) on concrete laser interactions.

spot_size.description: Beam diameter range for cleaning; smaller (100-500 μm) for fine details like cracks, larger (1-5 mm) for broad surface prep. Recommended 1000 μm (1 mm) for uniform coverage on rough concrete surfaces. Source: Equipment specifications from Coherent and Trumpf lasers; process optimization in Lasers Manuf. Mater. Process. (2020).

pass_count.description: Single pass for light contamination, multi-pass (3-5) for heavy layers to ensure complete removal without excessive energy input. Recommended 3 passes for typical industrial concrete cleaning to achieve uniform results. Source: Industry case studies in Constr. Build. Mater. (2019) on laser surface preparation.

overlap_ratio.description: Standard overlap for uniform scan coverage; 30-50% for faster large-area cleaning, 50-80% for detailed work to avoid gaps on uneven concrete. Recommended 50% for balanced efficiency and homogeneity. Source: Process optimization studies in J. Laser Appl. (2021) and manufacturer guidelines from Adapt Laser Systems.
